bugs.title: Shared view does not include bookings
bugs.description: The shared trip view at GET /shared/{token} returns trip info and itinerary but does not include any bookings. A ryokan booking was ingested and confirmed via ListBookings, but the shared view response has no 'bookings' key or it is empty. This means someone viewing a shared trip cannot see hotel/flight information, reducing the usefulness of shared views for trip coordination.
bugs.expected: Shared view includes bookings array with at least the ingested ryokan booking
bugs.actual: Shared view response has only 'trip' and 'itinerary' keys, no bookings

bugs.title: First planning message returned 'no response from AI' error
bugs.description: The first planning message asking for a day-by-day itinerary returned a textDelta with initial text, then an error event: {"error":{"message":"no response from AI — please try again"}}. The tool call was never made. A retry of the same message succeeded. This suggests a transient AI provider timeout or tool loop failure on the first attempt.
bugs.expected: AI completes the response and calls create_itinerary_items
bugs.actual: Partial text response followed by error: 'no response from AI — please try again'
bugs.api_command: buf curl ... SendMessage with itinerary request

ux_issues.description: Shared view itinerary uses snake_case field names (day_number) while the authenticated GetItinerary RPC uses camelCase (dayNumber). This inconsistency could cause frontend bugs if the same rendering logic is used for both views.
ux_issues.suggestion: Normalize field naming convention between shared view REST endpoint and ConnectRPC itinerary response

tool_failures.input: Build me a day-by-day itinerary with 2 days in Tokyo, a day trip to Hakone, and 4 days in Kyoto.
tool_failures.error: First attempt: partial text response then error 'no response from AI — please try again'. Second attempt succeeded.

usefulness_evaluation.narrative: The trip sharing lifecycle works correctly and securely. Token revocation is properly enforced (old tokens return 404 after unshare), re-sharing generates new tokens, and old tokens remain invalid after re-sharing. The shared view contains full itinerary content with day numbers, item titles, and location references. No private data leaks detected. The only gaps are: bookings are not included in shared views (P2), and the AI had one transient failure on the first planning message (retry worked).
